# User Device Settings Vectors

These vectors characterize SDK-level user device settings mapping under current shared ownership planning. They focus on pure model/protobuf behavior and avoid BLE transport setup so Android, iOS, and future common tests can share the same contract cases. `UserDeviceSettingsCommonPolicyTest.kt` now executes shared protobuf presence preservation, nullable omitted optionals, writable field serialization, encoder-owned trusted `lastModified`, explicit telemetry write policy, platform default divergence, and `sdk/user-device-settings/settings-model-readiness.json`/`user-device-settings-model-readiness` behavior-family coverage through `userDeviceSettingsModelReadinessManifestNamesEverySharedContractBehaviorFamily` for `protobuf-presence-preservation`, `nullable-omitted-optional-settings`, `writable-settings-serialization`, `encoder-owned-trusted-last-modified`, `explicit-telemetry-write-policy`, `platform-default-divergence`, `platform-user-device-settings-vector-references`, and the `compile-verification-gate` before production user-device-settings model code moves.
